rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THEODORE F SCHROEDER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Non-Parties' Amended Petition To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015, Line 6, Other Pinterest, Inc., Ben Silbermann, Michele Digiacomo, Evan Sharp, Paul Sciarra, Justin Edmund Hearing on Non-PArties Amended Petition to Quash. (Part 1 of 2) Granted in part and denied in part. As to the Subpoena to Pinterest, Inc./Ben Silbermann, by category of document: numbers 1-4: Modified. All document requests are limited to communications between Brian S. Cohen and any other person that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Numbers 5 - 9: Granted. Number 10: Modified. All documents that refer or relate to www.rendezvoo.com, RDV Version 2, the "Launchbed" platform, and Skoopwire.com from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Numbers 11-25: Granted. Number 26: Modified. All final document preservation mandates, policies or procedures from inception of the company to December 26, 2012. Pinterest, Inc. shall present a witness to appear at a deposition pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure § 2025.290. The deposition shall be completed on one calendar day. As to the Subpoena to Ben Silbermann, by category of document: Numbers 1-5, 7-8: Granted. Number 6: Modified. All documents that refer or relate to Theodore Schroeder, Brandon Stroy (Compl., 12), William Bocra (Compl., 14), RendezVoo (Compl., 14), Skoopwire (Compl., 37) from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Ben Silbermann shall appear for a deposition pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure § 2025.290. The deposition shall be completed on one calendar day. As to the Subpoena to Michelle DiGiacomo: Granted in its entirety. Michelle DiGiacomo shall not appear at her deposition. As to the Subpoena to Evan Sharp, by category of document: Number 1: Modified. All communications between Evan Sharp and Brian S. Cohen that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Number 2: granted with modification. All communications between Evan Sharp and any other principal of Pinterest that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Number 3: granted with modification. All communications between Evan Sharp and Vikram Bhaskaran that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Number 4: granted with modification. All communications between Evan Sharp and Justin Edmund that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Numbers 5-15: Granted. Evan Sharp shall appear for a deposition pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure § 2025.290. The deposition shall be completed on one calendar day. As to the Subpoena to Paul Sciarra, by category of document: Number 1: granted with modification. All communications between Paul Sciarra and Brian S. Cohen that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Number 2: Modified: All communications between Paul Sciarra and any other principal of Pinterest that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Number 3: Modified: All communications between Paul Sciarra and Vikram Bhaskaran that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Number 4: Modified: All communications between Paul Sciarra and Justin Edmund that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. Numbers 5-15: Granted. Paul Sciarra shall appear for a deposition pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure § 2025.290. The deposition shall be completed on one calendar day. As to the Subpoena to Justin Edmund, by category of document: Number 1: Modified: All communications between Justin Edmund and Brian S. Cohen that refer or relate to any of the plaintiffs from February 1, 2009 to December 26, 2012. (Continued to Part 2)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C. Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Third Amended Complaint. Denied. "It has long been the rule in California that a party waives any objection to the court's exercise of personal jurisdiction when the party makes a general appearance in the action." (Air Machine Com SRL v. Sup. Ct. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 414, 419.) (Id. at 420.) "[I]f a defendant seeks any affirmative relief on the merits, the application may be deemed a general appearance." (Dial 800 v. Fesbinder (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 32.) Defendant Bradshaw and Assoc., P.C., generally appeared by moving to compel Plaintiff to produce documents. Defendant appeared in Court and argued in favor of their motion to compel on November 12, 2014 and November 25, 2014, after the Third Amended Complaint was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MARTINA M. IBARRA et al VS. 39-41 LAKE STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 39-41 Lake Street Homeowners Association'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015, Line 17, Defendant 39-41 Lake Street Homeowners Association's Demurrer To First Amended Complaint. The Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled for failure to comply with Civ. Code § 5950. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first four causes of action. Plaintiff has ten days leave to amend to clarify in what capacity these claims are asserted against Defendant. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action, because the allegations made do not support a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MABEL LIN VS. R.J. MCGLENNON COMPANY, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MABEL LIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Two). Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Mabel Lin ("Lin") Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Production of Documents (Set Two) is GRANTED as unopposed. Since Lin has declined to request sanctions, notice sanctions, or argue for sanctions, therefore sanctions are DENIED. Lin shall prepare a form of the order consistent with this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: Fernandez v. Regents of the University of California San Francisco et al.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Regents Of The University Of California, San Francisco, Tracey Ann Mclean, M.D., Suha Patel, M.D., Juan M. Gonzalez-Velez, M.D., Julia Bruckner Newman, M.D., Gisela Alda Villagomez, M.D., Dana Henry, M.D. And Rachel Ruskin, M.D.'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015, Line 15, Defendants Regents Of The University Of California, San Francisco, Tracey Ann Mclean, M.D., Suha Patel, M.D., Juan M. Gonzalez-Velez, M.D., Julia Bruckner Newman, M.D., Gisela Alda Villagomez, M.D., Dana Henry, M.D. And Rachel Ruskin, M.D.'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Defendants' Demurrer is sustained. First, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is uncertain. The only allegation against Defendant Regents of the University of California is that it owned, operated and maintained medical facilities. This is not a basis for liability under any cause of action. Additionally, the FAC names multiple doctors as defendants but makes no specific allegations as to what treatment was provided to which Plaintiff that was allegedly negligent. As a result of these deficient pleadings, the Defendants have not been given sufficient notice of the allegations against them and are unable to meaningfully respond. Second, the FAC fails to allege in what capacity Plaintiff Christopher Calderon asserts the cause of action for negligence. Finally, the FAC fails to allege the required elements of a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiffs have ten days leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
